records.ety: [L. palpitare, palpitatum, v. intens. fr. pappare. See Palpable.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Palpitated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Palpitating (?).]

defs: To beat rapidly and more strongly than usual; to throb; to bound with emotion or exertion; to pulsate violently; to flutter; -- said specifically of the heart when its action is abnormal, as from excitement.
